D1.name: Easy Run
D1.description: Light mobility work, 5-10 minutes. Run, easy effort, 40 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Stretch, 5 minutes. Aim to hold a consistent heart rate and pace — don't go out too hard. You should finish feeling good.

D2.name: Steady Swim
D2.description: Don't worry about speed, work on technique and count your strokes on every third 50m of your 200s. Write it down to compare to future sessions.

D3.name: Easy Bike
D3.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes at 90 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes at 80 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 55 minutes at 90-95 RPM. Bike, very easy, 5 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. You can do this on road or trainer. Focus on smooth pedaling.

D4.name: Tech Swim
D4.description: Try doing 'catch up' as your drill and alternate your kicking with and without a board.

D5.name: Easy Run
D5.description: Run, easy effort, 40 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Optional: Work on weakest discipline — Easy Bike 70 or Steady Swim 1.8. Work on technique. Don't worry about speed.

D6.name: Bike/Run Brick
D6.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 80 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 55 minutes. Bike, very easy, 5 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Remember to hydrate during the session. Straight off the bike and into the run: Run, easy effort, 30 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Keep RPM up when running off bike — aim for around 93-95 RPM.

D7.name: Rest
D7.description: Moveable day off. Take the day off to recover from your week. Ideally rest on this day, but move your rest day if something else works better with your week. Do some light stretching or get a massage if you can.

D1.name: Steady State Swim
D1.description: Count your strokes on every third 50m of your 200s and aim to swim the same speed as last week taking 1 or 2 fewer strokes.

D2.name: Long Run
D2.description: Run, easy effort, 60 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Stretch, 5-10 minutes. Keep intensity even throughout the session. This will help build your base endurance which will support your higher intensity work later on. Keep RPM high and stand tall.

D3.name: Rest
D3.description: Moveable day off. Ideally rest on this day, but move your rest day if something else works better with your week.

D4.name: Test Swim
D4.description: Don't go out too hard. Take your time and heart rate at the end of the time trial swim.

D5.name: Build Run
D5.description: Core strength exercises, 15 minutes. Run, easy effort, 20 minutes. Run, moderate effort, 15 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 10 minutes. Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Stretch, 10 minutes. Build your pace throughout the session and maintain your form.

D6.name: Bike/Run Brick
D6.description: Bike, easy effort, 55 minutes. Bike, moderate effort, 20 minutes. Repeat 2 times. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Keep cadence around 90-95 RPM. Ride on hilly terrain. Stay in seat over hills and pedal smoothly. Aim for 30-50 grams of carbs per hour. Straight off the bike and into the run: Run, easy run, 20 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes.

D7.name: Steady Bike
D7.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, single leg, easy effort, 3 minutes, 90 RPM, swap legs every 30 seconds. Bike, moderate effort, 8 minutes, 75 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 2 minutes, 85 RPM. Repeat 3 times. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Do this session on the trainer or a set of rollers.

D1.name: Tempo Swim
D1.description: Aim for your target race speed on the 300s.

D2.name: Tech Run
D2.description: Run, easy effort, 10 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 2 minutes. Run, easy effort, 1 minute. Repeat 6 times. Run, easy effort, 8 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Work on technique during the pace variation. Maintain high RPM, stand tall and pick your knees up whether running fast or slow.

D3.name: Test Bike
D3.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Bike, time trial, best effort, 16km — record time and heart rate. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Try to use the same location for subsequent tests.

D4.name: Tech Swim
D4.description: Work on your recovery and catch at front of stroke.

D5.name: Rest
D5.description: Moveable day off. Ideally rest on this day. Do some light stretching or get a massage if you can.

D6.name: Build Run
D6.description: Run, easy effort, 20 minutes. Run, moderate effort, 15 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 10 minutes. Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Stretch, 10 minutes. Aim to maintain the same pace as the build run the week before but slightly longer.

D7.name: Long Bike
D7.description: Bike, easy effort, 55 minutes. Bike, moderate effort, 20 minutes. Repeat 2 times. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Long easy to steady ride on flat to hilly terrain. Focus on cadence 90-95 and efficiency. Remember to eat and drink during the ride. Ride with a group if you can.

D1.name: Easy Swim
D1.description: Use this session to loosen the legs from the past couple of days. Relax in the water and work on your technique.

D2.name: Hard Bike
D2.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Bike, high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Repeat 5 times. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes, 90 RPM. Cool down, 5-10 minutes.

D3.name: Easy Run
D3.description: Run, easy effort, 40 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Optional: Work on weakest discipline — Easy Bike 70 or Steady Swim 1.8.

D4.name: Test Run
D4.description: Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Run, moderate effort, 3 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 2 minutes. Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Run, time trial, best effort, 3km — record time and heart rate. Run, easy effort, 10 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Run on a flat surface. Don't go out too hard, aim to build through the effort.

D5.name: Steady Swim
D5.description: Work on your stroke during the pull set, then maintain the form when you introduce your kick.

D6.name: Bike/Run Brick + Tempo Run
D6.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Bike, med-high effort, 40 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes. Straight off the bike and into the run: Run, med-high effort, 20 minutes. Run, easy effort, 10 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Work on form during your run.

D7.name: Rest
D7.description: Moveable day off. Try not to do back-to-back hard sessions. Work on your nutrition — especially recovery after hard sessions: carbs and protein within 30 minutes of finishing.

D1.name: Tempo Swim
D1.description: Aim for your target race speed on the 300s.

D2.name: Easy Run
D2.description: Light mobility work, 5-10 minutes. Run, easy effort, 40 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Stretch, 5 minutes. Aim to hold a consistent heart rate and pace. You should finish feeling good.

D3.name: Steady Swim
D3.description: Aim to hold the same speeds relative to the Steady Swim session last week where you were doing 100m reps.

D4.name: Rest
D4.description: Rest day. Consider getting a proper bike fit if you haven't — it improves comfort, efficiency, and your run off the bike.

D5.name: Hard Run
D5.description: Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Run, moderate effort, 5 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 5 minutes. Recovery run, easy effort, 2 minutes. Run, high effort, 6 minutes. Recovery run, easy effort, 2 minutes. Repeat 3 times. Run, easy effort, 9 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Aim to be as fast on your third rep as your first. Keep cadence high even when tiring.

D6.name: Hilly Bike
D6.description: Bike, easy effort, 30 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 20 minutes, 75 RPM. Repeat 3 times. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Long ride over hilly terrain. Pedal smoothly over hills. Remember to hydrate and fuel.

D7.name: Steady OW Swim
D7.description: Swim in open water to start getting used to that environment. Swim, easy effort, 5 minutes. Swim, breathe bilaterally if you can, moderate effort, 8 minutes. Recovery, 2 minutes. Repeat 3 times. Swim and kick, easy effort, 5 minutes. Nice and relaxed. Enjoy!

D1.name: Rest
D1.description: Rest day. Recovery week. Massage or light stretching. Focus on glutes, quads and hamstrings.

D2.name: Tech Swim
D2.description: See if you can get a swim coach to look at your stroke and give you tips on your technique.

D3.name: Easy Run
D3.description: Light mobility work, 5-10 minutes. Run, easy effort, 50 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Keep working on your technique even when running slowly. Don't get sloppy — pick up knees, stand tall, keep RPM around 93-95.

D4.name: Rest
D4.description: Rest day. Enjoy sleeping in and do some light stretching or yoga in the evening.

D5.name: Easy Run
D5.description: Run, easy effort, 40 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Optional: Work on weakest discipline — Easy Bike 70 or Steady Swim 1.8.

D6.name: Bike/Run Brick + Tempo Run
D6.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Bike, med-high effort, 40 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes. Straight off the bike and into the run: Run, med-high effort, 20 minutes. Run, easy effort, 10 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Work on your form during the run.

D7.name: Long Run
D7.description: Run, easy effort, 80 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Stretch, 5-10 minutes. Keep intensity even throughout. Keep RPM high, stand tall. Congratulations — you're halfway through your preparation. Stay consistent.

D1.name: Easy Swim
D1.description: Use this session to loosen the legs. Relax in the water and work on your technique.

D2.name: Fartlek Run
D2.description: Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Run, moderate effort, 4 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 3 minutes. Recovery run, 2 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 2 minutes. Run, easy effort, 1 minute. Run, high effort, 1 minute. Run, easy effort, 1 minute. Repeat 6 times. Run, easy effort, 6 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Make sure you have your recovery nutrition in place before starting this one.

D3.name: Easy Bike
D3.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes at 90 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes at 80 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 55 minutes at 90-95 RPM. Bike, very easy, 5 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Focus on smooth pedaling.

D4.name: Tempo Swim
D4.description: Aim for your target race speed on the 300s.

D5.name: Hilly Bike
D5.description: Bike, easy effort, 30 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 20 minutes, 75 RPM. Repeat 3 times. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Stay in the seat over the hills and practice pedaling in a full circle — no dead spots.

D6.name: Long Run
D6.description: Run, easy effort, 80 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Slight increase in length. Keep working on technique. Take a couple of gels — one at 20 minutes, one at 40 minutes.

D7.name: Rest
D7.description: Moveable day off. Ideally rest on this day. Do some light stretching or get a massage.

D1.name: Steady Swim
D1.description: Slightly longer reps to improve endurance. Keep counting strokes every third 50m to check you are holding your form together.

D2.name: Steady Bike
D2.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, single leg, easy effort, 3 minutes, 90 RPM, swap legs every 30 seconds. Bike, moderate effort, 10 minutes, 75 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 2 minutes, 85 RPM. Repeat 4 times. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Do this session on the trainer or a set of rollers.

D3.name: Build Run
D3.description: Run, easy effort, 20 minutes. Run, moderate effort, 15 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 10 minutes. Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Use same course as in week 3. You should see an increase in distance for same effort.

D4.name: Rest
D4.description: Moveable day off. Do some light stretching or get a massage. Start thinking about race equipment — now is a good time to get organised.

D5.name: Tempo Bike
D5.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Bike, med-high effort, 10 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 2 minutes, 85 RPM. Repeat 5 times. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes, 90 RPM. Cool down, 5-10 minutes.

D6.name: Steady OW Swim
D6.description: Swim in open water. Swim, easy effort, 5 minutes. Swim, moderate effort, 1800m. Swim and kick, easy effort, 10 minutes. Use the goggles and wetsuit you intend to race in. Practice taking your wetsuit off quickly at the end.

D7.name: Steady Run
D7.description: Run, moderate effort, 70 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Stretch, 5-10 minutes. Slight increase in intensity — getting used to pushing at higher intensity for longer. Keep RPM high throughout and stand tall.

D1.name: Rest
D1.description: Moveable day off. Recovery and testing week — take it easy.

D2.name: Easy Run
D2.description: Light mobility work, 5-10 minutes. Run, easy effort, 40 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. If legs are sore, you could do this session as an aqua jog at the pool.

D3.name: Test Swim
D3.description: Compare with the test in week 2. Control your intensity to start.

D4.name: Easy Bike
D4.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 80 RPM. Bike, max effort, 15 seconds, 100-105 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 4 minutes 45 seconds, 90 RPM. Repeat 8 times. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. The sprints will help to wake up your legs.

D5.name: Build Swim
D5.description: Hold your technique together as you increase your speed. Remember to relax your arm during the recovery phase.

D6.name: Easy Run
D6.description: Run, easy effort, 40 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Optional: Work on weakest discipline — Easy Bike 70 minutes or Steady Swim 1.8.

D7.name: Test Bike
D7.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Bike, time trial, best effort, 16km — record time and heart rate. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Use the same location as your test in week 3.

D1.name: Tech Swim
D1.description: Work on your recovery and catch at the front of your stroke.

D2.name: Easy Bike
D2.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 80 RPM. Bike, max effort, 15 seconds, 100-105 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 4 minutes 45 seconds, 90 RPM. Repeat 8 times. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Can be done on road or trainer. The sprints will help wake up your legs.

D3.name: Test Run
D3.description: Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Run, moderate effort, 3 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 2 minutes. Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Run, time trial, best effort, 3km — record time and heart rate. Run, easy effort, 10 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Use the same location as week 4 test. Don't go out too hard, pace your effort.

D4.name: Rest
D4.description: Rest day. Enjoy sleeping in and do some light stretching or yoga in the evening. Book your bike in to be serviced now, so it's not a last minute rush before you race.

D5.name: Tempo Swim
D5.description: Use the 1500m rep to practice your pacing for your race. Visualize yourself swimming in the event.

D6.name: Race Sim Brick + Tempo Run
D6.description: Race simulation brick — use race kit. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Bike, med-high effort, 30km, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes. Straight off the bike into the run: Run, med-high effort, 7km. Run, easy effort, 10 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Use this session to practice pacing for the race.

D7.name: Easy Run
D7.description: Run, easy effort, 40 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Optional: If still feeling tired, take another day off. Otherwise, work on your weakest discipline.

D1.name: Fartlek Run
D1.description: Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Run, moderate effort, 4 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 3 minutes. Recovery run, 2 minutes. Run, med-high effort, 2 minutes. Run, easy effort, 1 minute. Run, high effort, 1 minute. Run, easy effort, 1 minute. Repeat 6 times. Run, easy effort, 6 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Don't force the speed — focus on technique and it will come naturally.

D2.name: Easy Swim
D2.description: Longer reps prepare you for your race. If racing in a wetsuit, use it for part or all of this session.

D3.name: Easy Run
D3.description: Run, easy effort, 40 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Optional: If still tired, take another day off. Otherwise, work on your weakest discipline.

D4.name: Hard Bike
D4.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Bike, high effort, 3 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 2 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, max effort, 30 seconds, 100 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 2 minutes, 85 RPM. Repeat 4 times. Bike, easy effort, 10 minutes, 90 RPM. Cool down, 5-10 minutes.

D5.name: Tempo Swim
D5.description: Use the 1500m rep to practice your pacing for your race. Visualize yourself swimming in the event.

D6.name: Bike/Run Brick + Tempo Run
D6.description: Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high effort, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes. Bike, med-high effort, 30 minutes, 95 RPM. Off the bike and straight into the run. Practice pacing, use race equipment. Run, med-high effort, 10 minutes. Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Repeat 2 times. Cool down, 5-10 minutes.

D7.name: Rest
D7.description: Rest day. All the hard work is done now. Start doing some visualization of your race to mentally prepare.

D1.name: Max Swim
D1.description: Final swim session. Race pace efforts. Feel the speed.

D2.name: Rest
D2.description: Rest day. Light stretching or massage. Write out a timeline from the evening before the race to race start — it helps you relax.

D3.name: Hard Bike
D3.description: Bike, easy effort, 3 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, moderate effort, 3 minutes, 90 RPM. Bike, med-high, 5 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 3 minutes. Bike, high effort, 2 minutes, 95 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 1 minute, 85 RPM. Bike, max effort, 30 seconds, 100 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 2 minutes, 85 RPM. Bike, easy effort, 5 minutes, 90 RPM. Cool down, 5-10 minutes.

D4.name: Rest
D4.description: Rest day. Start organizing all your race kit in one place. Take spares of essential equipment — goggles, laces, inner tubes, tire.

D5.name: Pick Up Run
D5.description: Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Run, high effort, 30 seconds. Run, moderate effort, 90 seconds. Repeat 5 times. Run, easy effort, 5 minutes. Cool down, 5-10 minutes. Run on a soft surface in race shoes. Don't force the pace — you should be feeling good, let the speed come naturally.

D6.name: Easy Swim + Easy Bike
D6.description: Easy Swim: short, relaxed, race prep. Easy Bike: Z2, 20 minutes, 95 RPM. Use the bike ride just to check gears and brakes — make sure everything works. Try to do both workouts in the morning and relax in the evening.

D7.name: RACE DAY
D7.description: Race Day! Good luck! Get there with plenty of time to spare. Use your Garmin as a guide but race on feel too. HR can be higher than normal due to adrenaline. Aim to even or negative split through each leg, just like you practiced. Most of all — enjoy yourself!
